description_long: This is a twin-engine, high-wing utility airplane with a straight, rectangular wing configuration and a conventional tail. The fuselage is painted primarily light gray with prominent orange markings on the wingtips, tail, and nose, and features roundels and insignia on the sides, suggesting a military or governmental use. The engines are mounted on the wings and each drives a multi-blade propeller. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the crew. The landing gear is not visible, but the overall design suggests a robust, utilitarian aircraft suitable for transport or patrol missions. The surface appears smooth and metallic, with minimal surface texture and a matte finish.
description: A twin-engine, high-wing utility airplane with orange markings and a conventional tail, featuring a forward cockpit and straight wings. The fuselage is light gray with military-style insignia and smooth, matte surfaces.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, high-mounted straight wings, twin propeller engines, and orange markings on the wingtips and tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wings, twin-engine nacelles, and the conventional tail with orange-tipped horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the propeller engines and side insignia.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the forward cockpit, nose cone with orange tip, and the symmetrical placement of the engines and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight rectangular wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; configuration cannot be determined from images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
